courseTitle: B2 TED TALKS第4堂 - 呼吸也會胖？Where Does Fat Go When You Lose Weight?

vocabulary.definition: relating to body fat; fatty tissue that stores energy
vocabulary.example: Adipose tissue is made up of fat cells called adipocytes.

vocabulary.definition: the process of burning/breaking down a substance by combining it with oxygen
vocabulary.example: In order to burn fat you have to perform oxidization on these molecules.

vocabulary.zh: 甘油（脂肪分子的組成成分）
vocabulary.definition: a sweet, syrup-like compound; one of the components of triglycerides
vocabulary.example: Triglycerides are made up of glycerol and three fatty acids.

vocabulary.definition: fat molecules stored in fat cells; high levels linked to heart disease
vocabulary.example: Fat cells are filled with molecules called triglycerides.

vocabulary.definition: a biological molecule that speeds up chemical reactions in the body
vocabulary.example: The complete oxidization of a single triglyceride involves many biochemical enzymes.

vocabulary.definition: the smallest particle of a substance that retains its properties
vocabulary.example: Fat cells are filled with molecules called triglycerides.

vocabulary.definition: relating to the chemical processes that occur in living organisms
vocabulary.example: High levels of triglycerides are linked with heart disease and metabolic syndrome.

vocabulary.definition: a phrase used to motivate and unite people toward a cause
vocabulary.example: "Feel the burn!" seems to be the rallying cry for exercise enthusiasts everywhere.

vocabulary.definition: a set of rules or instructions for a computer to follow
vocabulary.example: An algorithm will decide what you should be eating!
8.4 kg of fat is exhaled as CO2.

vocabulary.definition: an exercise machine for walking or running in place
vocabulary.example: Even without hitting the treadmill, with every breath we take, we give off 33g of CO2.

vocabulary.definition: to use part of a stored supply or reserve
vocabulary.example: Your body has to use up the immediate energy from food, and dip into your energy reserves.

vocabulary.definition: to be reducible to; to have something as the most essential point
vocabulary.example: The process can be boiled down to a single chemical equation.
A small amount of food can quickly make all that exercise for naught.

sentences.sentence: When you eat too much protein or carbs, your body converts it and stores it as fat.
sentences.zh: 當你攝取過多蛋白質或碳水化合物，身體會將其轉化並以脂肪的形式儲存。

sentences.sentence: According to a study published in the British Medical Journal, most of the fat turns into carbon dioxide and water.
sentences.zh: 根據英國醫學期刊的一項研究，大部分脂肪會轉化成二氧化碳和水。

sentences.sentence: Most of the mass is breathed out as carbon dioxide — it goes into thin air.
sentences.zh: 大部分的質量以二氧化碳的形式被呼出——它就這樣消失在空氣中。

sentences.sentence: Not only will your breath keep track of how much fat you're burning, but an algorithm will decide what you should be eating!
sentences.zh: 不只你的呼吸能追蹤你燃燒了多少脂肪，演算法還能決定你該吃什麼！

sentences.sentence: A single muffin contains 20% of your daily intake of energy.
sentences.zh: 一個小鬆餅就含有你每日能量攝取的 20%。

grammar.rule: Not only...but (also)... 不只⋯也⋯（強調追加）
grammar.explanation: 強調除了已知的影響之外，還有另一個（通常更令人意外的）結果。注意：not only 放句首時，主詞和動詞要倒裝。
examples: Not only will your breath keep track of how much fat you're burning, but an algorithm will decide what you should be eating!
Sitting doesn't just increase your risk for obesity, but it can also raise your risk for dementia.

grammar.rule: Be + past participle + as (被動語態：以⋯形式釋放/轉化)
grammar.explanation: 表達「某物以某種形式被釋放、排出或測量」，強調結果而非誰做了這個動作。
examples: 84% of an adipose triglyceride is given off as CO2.
The rest is given off as water in some way or another.

topicSummary: 這堂課的影片要解答一個你可能從沒想過的問題：減肥的時候，脂肪到底去哪了？很多人以為脂肪會變成熱能，或隨汗水排出——但科學的答案出乎意料：大部分脂肪其實是以二氧化碳的形式，透過呼吸被你吐出去的！影片也說明了為什麼「運動完吃一個小鬆餅，就能輕易抵消你的所有努力」，讓你對飲食和運動有全新的認識。
discussion: 你以前以為脂肪消失的方式是什麼？（例如：變成熱能、隨汗水排出）
聽到「脂肪主要是靠呼吸排出的」，你第一個反應是什麼？
影片說一個小鬆餅就含有你每日能量的 20%，這讓你對飲食有什麼新的想法？
你平常怎麼管理飲食和運動的平衡？有沒有因為某個科學知識而改變習慣？
